basics.description: Deploy the **AI Foundry Landing Zone Accelerator** (FinOps Edition). Pick a blueprint, optionally wire to an existing ALZ hub, and review before deploy. Reference architecture: [github.com/kimvaddi/ai-foundry-landing-zone-accelerator](https://github.com/kimvaddi/ai-foundry-landing-zone-accelerator).

validations.message: Owner or Contributor on the subscription is required (this template creates resource groups).

location.label: Primary region
location.toolTip: Region for the foundation (LAW, App Insights, Key Vault) and Foundry account. AI Search location can be overridden in the next step.

basics.name: workload
basics.label: Workload short name
basics.toolTip: 3-8 lower-case chars. Used in every resource name (e.g. log-<workload>-<env>-<suffix>).

constraints.validationMessage: Lower-case letters/digits, 3-8 chars, starts with a letter.

basics.name: env
basics.label: Environment
basics.toolTip: Environment label baked into resource names and the `env` tag.

basics.name: costCenter
basics.label: Cost center tag
basics.toolTip: Stamped onto the `costCenter` resource tag. The FinOps showback workbook groups spend by this tag.

options.text: A blueprint pre-fills sane defaults for compute, APIM, and safety toggles. You can override any of them in the later steps.

elements.name: blueprintPicker
elements.label: Blueprint
elements.toolTip: Pick a starting profile. See the README cost-guidance table for monthly cost estimates per blueprint.

allowedValues.label: poc-standalone-spoke (~$70/mo — adds spoke VNet + 21 PDNS + PEs)
poc-hub-connected (~$45/mo — peers to your hub, reuses central PDNS)

elements.name: networkMode
elements.label: Network mode
elements.defaultValue: standalone (greenfield) — module creates the spoke VNet + DNS
elements.toolTip: standalone = the module owns the spoke VNet, 21 private DNS zones, NSGs, route tables. hub-connected = peer to an existing ALZ hub, reuse the hub's central PDNS zones.

allowedValues.label: standalone (greenfield) — module creates the spoke VNet + DNS
hub-connected (brownfield) — peer to existing ALZ hub

elements.name: vnetAddressSpace
elements.label: Spoke VNet address space
elements.toolTip: /20 fits all 9 subnets with room. Must not overlap with your hub's address space.

elements.name: searchLocation
elements.label: AI Search region (override)
elements.toolTip: Leave blank to co-locate with the primary region. Override only if you turn `enforceApimChokepoint` off (cross-region PE not allowed).

options.text: Hub-connected mode requires an existing ALZ-style hub. You MUST supply the hub VNet resource ID and the resource IDs of the central private DNS zones the spoke will link to.

elements.name: hubVnetResourceId
elements.label: Hub VNet resource ID
elements.toolTip: /subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<name>

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a full ARM resource ID for a Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks resource.

elements.name: hubFirewallPrivateIp
elements.label: Hub Azure Firewall private IP
elements.toolTip: Required when forced tunneling is on. Becomes the next-hop in the 0.0.0.0/0 UDR.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or enter a valid IPv4 address.

elements.name: enableForcedTunneling
elements.label: Enable forced tunneling (0/0 → hub firewall)
elements.toolTip: Day-0 recommendation: leave OFF, validate peering + DNS, then flip ON once hub firewall rules are in place.

elements.name: createReverseHubPeer
elements.label: Create reverse hub → spoke peering
elements.toolTip: Only works for same-subscription hubs. Requires write permission on the hub resource group.

options.text: Paste the resource IDs of the central private DNS zones in your hub. The spoke will link its private endpoints to these zones (instead of creating new ones).

elements.name: pdnsVaultcore
elements.label: privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net resource ID
elements.toolTip: /subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net

elements.name: pdnsApim
elements.label: privatelink.azure-api.net resource ID (APIM)
elements.toolTip: Only required if you deploy APIM in `internal` mode.

elements.name: pdnsCosmos
elements.label: privatelink.documents.azure.com resource ID (Cosmos)
elements.toolTip: Only required if a Foundry project enables Agent Service capability host.

options.text: Defaults below reflect the selected blueprint. Toggle to override. AI Search is always on.

elements.name: apimDeploy
elements.label: Deploy APIM AI Gateway
elements.toolTip: Provisions an APIM StandardV2 instance with token-limit, semantic-cache, content-safety, and emit-token-metric policies. Required for the chokepoint pattern.

elements.name: enforceApimChokepoint
elements.label: Enforce APIM as the SINGLE chokepoint
elements.toolTip: Sets Foundry + Search publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, attaches a Search PE, and locks NSGs so ONLY the APIM subnet can reach the PE subnet. Recommended enterprise default. Requires APIM in internal/external mode.

elements.name: bastionDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Azure Bastion
elements.toolTip: Standard SKU Bastion in the spoke. Required to RDP/SSH into the jump or build VMs.

elements.name: jumpvmDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Windows jump VM
elements.toolTip: Standard_B2s Windows VM in the JumpboxSubnet. Reached via Bastion.

elements.name: buildvmDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Linux build VM (CI runner)
elements.toolTip: Standard_B2s Linux VM in the BuildAgentSubnet. SSH key required.

elements.name: appGatewayDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Application Gateway (WAF_v2)
elements.toolTip: WAF v2 in front of APIM (or your app). Public IP + managed cert.

elements.name: caeDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Container Apps Environment
elements.toolTip: Internal CAE in the ContainerAppEnvironmentSubnet. Hosts agent runtimes.

elements.name: notificationsDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Teams notifications (Logic App)
elements.toolTip: Logic App that posts an Adaptive Card to a Teams channel via Workflows Incoming Webhook. Ships disabled.

elements.name: enableFoundryAgentInjection
elements.label: Enable Foundry Agent Service injection
elements.toolTip: Registers the AIFoundrySubnet on the Foundry account so the Standard Agent Service can inject. Requires AKS GPU SKU capacity in the chosen region.

elements.name: createFoundryCapabilityHost
elements.label: Create Foundry account-level capability host (Agents)
elements.toolTip: Required for the Standard Agent Service to host agents. Implies agent injection.

options.text: These secrets are passed via Secure Strings — never logged. They are required when the corresponding VM is deployed.

elements.name: jumpvmAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Local admin password for the Windows jump VM. Min 12 chars, 3 of 4 categories (upper, lower, digit, symbol).

constraints.validationMessage: Min 12 chars, must include 3 of: upper, lower, digit, symbol.

elements.name: buildvmSshPublicKey
elements.label: Build VM SSH public key
elements.toolTip: OpenSSH single-line format. Generate with: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C build-vm

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid OpenSSH public key line (ssh-rsa / ssh-ed25519 / ecdsa-sha2-*).

options.text: APIM publisher details + the AI-Gateway safety/cache policies that get applied to the foundry-default product.

elements.name: apimProductTokensPerMinute
elements.label: Token rate limit (TPM) on foundry-default product
elements.toolTip: Templated into product-token-limit.xml. Above this, APIM returns 429.

elements.name: enableSemanticCache
elements.label: Enable APIM semantic cache (provisions Azure Managed Redis)
elements.toolTip: Provisions Azure Managed Redis (Balanced_B0) + APIM external cache + embeddings backend. ~60-80% cost savings on repeat prompts.

elements.name: embeddingsDeploymentName
elements.label: Embeddings deployment name (for semantic cache)
elements.toolTip: Must match a model deployment configured below.

options.text: These are passed as JSON arrays directly to the template. Edit in place — the defaults reflect the selected blueprint.

elements.name: modelDeployments
elements.label: Model deployments (JSON array)
elements.toolTip: Array of { name, model:{format,name,version}, sku:{name,capacity} } objects.

elements.name: foundryProjects
elements.label: Foundry projects (JSON array)
elements.defaultValue: [if(startsWith(steps('blueprint').blueprintPicker, 'prod-'), '[{"name":"platform","displayName":"Platform team","description":"Foundry workspace for the central AI platform team."},{"name":"business-unit-a","displayName":"Business unit A","description":"Isolated workspace for BU-A apps and agents."}]', '[{"name":"default","displayName":"Default project","description":"Foundry project."}]')]
elements.toolTip: Array of { name, displayName, description } objects.

options.text: Optional — leave blank to skip. The Logic App always ships in state=Disabled; you can flip it on after wiring the webhook.

elements.name: enableNotificationsLogicApp
elements.label: Set Logic App state to Enabled on deploy

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.toolTip: From Teams channel → Workflows → 'Post to a channel when a webhook request is received'. SecureString — never logged.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Teams workflow webhook URL.

elements.name: notificationEmails
elements.label: Fallback notification emails (comma-separated)
elements.toolTip: Optional. e.g. ai-platform@contoso.com,secops@contoso.com

outputs.existingPrivateDnsZones: [if(equals(steps('blueprint').networkMode, 'hub-connected'), createObject('vaultcore', steps('hub').pdnsVaultcore, 'openai', steps('hub').pdnsOpenai, 'cognitiveServices', steps('hub').pdnsCognitive, 'search', steps('hub').pdnsSearch, 'blob', steps('hub').pdnsBlob, 'apim', steps('hub').pdnsApim, 'cosmosSql', steps('hub').pdnsCosmos), createObject())]
outputs.components: [createObject('bastion', createObject('deploy', steps('components').bastionDeploy, 'sku', 'Standard'), 'jumpvm', createObject('deploy', steps('components').jumpvmDeploy, 'sku', 'Standard_B2s'), 'buildvm', createObject('deploy', steps('components').buildvmDeploy, 'sku', 'Standard_B2s'), 'appGateway', createObject('deploy', steps('components').appGatewayDeploy, 'wafEnabled', true, 'sku', 'WAF_v2'), 'containerAppsEnv', createObject('deploy', steps('components').caeDeploy), 'apim', createObject('deploy', steps('components').apimDeploy, 'sku', 'StandardV2', 'networkMode', if(steps('components').apimDeploy, steps('components').apimNetworkMode, 'none')), 'standaloneSearch', createObject('deploy', true, 'sku', steps('components').standaloneSearchSku), 'notifications', createObject('deploy', steps('components').notificationsDeploy), 'otelCollector', createObject('deploy', false))]
